summary: App crashes (native SIGSEGV) when saving edits to a saved place that was removed from another list
description: On the AAOS head unit, com.example.tripmarks terminates with a native crash (signal 11, SIGSEGV) the moment the user taps Save in the edit-place dialog. Repro: open a saved place from list A, then in list B delete that place (or clear/empty its list) while the edit dialog for it is still open, switch back and change its name or color and tap Save. The app dies immediately on the main thread. The tombstone shows the crash crossing the JNI boundary: the Java call path goes Bookmark.updateParams -> the native method nativeUpdateParams, and the native backtrace faults inside the app's own libmapcore.so right after the JNI trampoline (Java_com_example_tripmarks_maps_bookmarks_data_Bookmark_nativeUpdateParams). fault addr is near-null (0x18), which looks like the native handle lookup returning no bookmark (the id is now stale) and the code then reading the bookmark's data off a null pointer. 100% reproducible when the place has been deleted underneath the open dialog; no repro when the place still exists. Debug builds assert on the missing bookmark; release builds crash. See attached tombstone.
